tactics.name: Reconnaissance
tactics.description: Gathering information to plan future adversary operations.

tactics.name: Initial Access
tactics.description: Techniques to gain initial foothold within a network.

tactics.name: Execution
tactics.description: Techniques resulting in adversary-controlled code running on a system.

tactics.name: Persistence
tactics.description: Techniques used to keep access to systems across restarts and credential changes.

tactics.name: Privilege Escalation
tactics.description: Techniques to gain higher-level permissions on a system or network.

tactics.name: Defense Evasion
tactics.description: Techniques to avoid detection throughout the compromise.

tactics.name: Credential Access
tactics.description: Stealing credentials such as account names and passwords.

tactics.name: Discovery
tactics.description: Techniques to gain knowledge about the system and internal network.

tactics.name: Lateral Movement
tactics.description: Techniques to enter and control remote systems on a network.

tactics.name: Command and Control
tactics.description: Techniques to communicate with compromised systems within a victim network.

tactics.name: Exfiltration
tactics.description: Techniques to steal data from your network.

tactics.name: Impact
tactics.description: Techniques to disrupt availability or compromise integrity.

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts.

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to take advantage of a weakness in an Internet-facing computer or program.

techniques.name: Remote Services: RDP
techniques.description: Adversaries may use RDP to log into systems.

techniques.name: Remote Services: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
techniques.description: Adversaries may use SMB to interact with file shares on remote systems.

techniques.name: Remote Services: SSH
techniques.description: Adversaries may use SSH to log into systems.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols associated with web traffic.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol: FTP
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using FTP to avoid detection.

techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access.

techniques.name: Unsecured Credentials
techniques.description: Adversaries may search compromised systems for credentials in cleartext.

techniques.name: Gather Victim Host Information
techniques.description: Adversaries may gather information about the victim's hosts.

techniques.name: Active Scanning
techniques.description: Adversaries may execute active reconnaissance scans to gather information.

techniques.name: External Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use remote services such as VPNs, RDP, and SSH to gain access.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts.

techniques.name: Network Sniffing
techniques.description: Adversaries may sniff network traffic to capture cleartext credentials.

techniques.name: Exploitation of Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may exploit remote services to gain unauthorized access.
